name: Franco's Pasta with Winter Squash and Potatoes
recipeIngredient: 3/4 pound squash, peeled and cut into 3/4-inch cubes - about 2 cups
1 large yellow-fleshed potato, peeled and cut into 3/4-inch cubes
2 -3 tablespoons salt plus coarse sea salt
1 small piece of hot red pepper or chili pepper, to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring water to a rolling boil.
2. Add the squash, potato, and salt.
3. Cook for 2 minutes, add the pasta, and cook until it offers considerable resistance to the tooth, about three quarters of the recommended cooking time.
4. While the pasta is cooking, put the garlic, hot pepper, and parsley in a large non-stick skillet and drizzle with 1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil. Place the skillet over moderate heat and when the garlic begins to sizzle, remove from heat.
5. Drain the pasta and vegetables, reserving 2 cups of the cooking water. Put the drained pasta and vegetables in the skillet with the garlic and add 1 cup of pasta cooking water. Cook until the pasta is cooked and surrounded by a creamy sauce. Add more cooking water if the sauce dries out.
6. Serve the pasta on bowls, topped with a drizzle of olive oil and cheese.
Yield: 4-6 servings
